passage.title: Station Route Redesign
passage.text: The trip planning team wanted younger students to follow a bus tour route more easily. At first, the team used one route map filled with tiny stop names and curved arrows, but students missed where to get off because the stop names were too hard to scan. A practice tour group said "I can read some names, but I still do not know my next stop." After hearing that, the team used one strong symbol for each travel stop and matched each stop sign with the same color stripe. They also hung a reminder sign near the bus door so students could confirm the next stop before leaving the bus. On the next test day, the younger group changed buses with fewer mistakes on the next practice trip. The team noticed that consistent travel clues helped more than many different sign styles. Because of that result, the trip team planned to use the same route style on future tours.

questions.prompt: What is the passage mainly about?
choices: Buying more books for a library sale
Writing long reports about class rules
Improving travel signs so younger students can follow a route
Painting a room with brighter colors
questions.rationale: The whole passage focuses on improving a system for younger students.

questions.prompt: What problem did students mention about the first plan?
choices: Students could not tell what to do next
The stop names were too hard to scan quickly
The labels were too small to read quickly
The first plan had too many steps
questions.rationale: The early part of the passage explains what did not work well.

questions.prompt: What did the team place at every transfer point?
choices: Mini maps
Larger arrows
Short signs
Color blocks
questions.rationale: The middle of the passage tells what the team added or changed.

questions.prompt: What does consistent mean in the seventh sentence?
choices: A room that is easy to clean
A rule about lunch time
A person who leads the school
Staying the same in a helpful way
questions.rationale: The word meaning comes from the way the passage uses it.

questions.prompt: Why did the new design work better for younger students?
choices: It reduced the number of students
It removed every sign from the area
The new travel clues matched each stop more clearly
It made the room much larger
questions.rationale: You have to connect the redesign changes with the later results.

questions.prompt: What will the team most likely do next?
choices: Ask students to memorize everything
Use the same route style on more school trips
Use the same clearer style in more places
Remove every sign from the room
questions.rationale: The last sentence points to the team's next likely action.
